spans.entity_input: {"prompt": "What is the current API for SeaORM's find_with_related and how does it differ from load_many for batch loading?"}

{"response": "Running tool [38;5;141mresolve-library-id[0m with the param[38;5;244m (from mcp server: context7)[0m

 ⋮  {
 ⋮    "libraryId": "/websites/rs_sea-orm_1_1_14",
 ⋮    "query": "find_with_related vs load_many batch loading related entities eager loading"
 ⋮  }
[0m[38;5;244m - Completed in 1.543s[0m

[38;5;141m> [0mHere's the comparison:[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m## [38;5;10mfind_with_related[0m — JOIN-based eager loading[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10m// Single query using LEFT JOIN
let results: Vec<(cake::Model, Vec<fruit::Model>)> = Cake::find()
    .find_with_related(Fruit)
    .all(&db)
    .await?;
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- Issues a [1msingle SQL query[22m with a [38;5;10mLEFT JOIN[0m between the two tables.[0m[0m
- Returns [38;5;10mVec<(ParentModel, Vec<ChildModel>)>[0m — already grouped.[0m[0m
- Works for both one-to-many and many-to-many (auto-joins through the junction table).[0m[0m
- Parent rows are [1mdeduplicated[22m client-side (a parent appears once even though the JOIN produces multiple rows).[0m[0m
- Orders by parent PK ascending.[0m[0m
- Downside: transfers redundant parent column data for every child row (the classic N+1-avoidance tradeoff).[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m## [38;5;10mload_many[0m (via [38;5;10mLoaderTrait[0m) — separate-query batch loading[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[1mrust
[0m[38;5;10m// Two queries: one you already ran, one WHERE...IN for children
let cakes: Vec<cake::Model> = Cake::find().all(&db).await?;

let fruits: Vec<Vec<fruit::Model>> = cakes
    .load_many(Fruit::find(), &db)
    .await?;
// fruits[i] corresponds to cakes[i]
[0m[0m[0m
[0m[0m
- Issues a [1mseparate SELECT[22m with a [38;5;10mWHERE foreign_key IN (...)[0m clause (deduplicates keys automatically).[0m[0m
- Returns [38;5;10mVec<Vec<ChildModel>>[0m — positionally aligned with the input slice.[0m[0m
- No data duplication across the wire; parent data isn't re-transmitted.[0m[0m
- You can [1mfilter/order the child query[22m by passing a customized [38;5;10mSelect[0m instead of [38;5;10mEntity::find()[0m.[0m[0m
- Better for wide parent rows or when you need children from [1mmultiple different relations[22m (call [38;5;10mload_many[0m / [38;5;10mload_one[0m multiple times on the same parent vec without re-joining).[0m[0m
[0m[0m
[38;5;252m[1m## When to use which[0m[0m
[0m[0m
| Concern | [38;5;10mfind_with_related[0m | [38;5;10mload_many[0m |[0m[0m
|---|---|---|[0m[0m
| Query count | 1 (JOIN) | 2 (parent + children) |[0m[0m
| Data duplication | Parent cols repeated per child row | None |[0m[0m
| Filtering children | Limited (shared WHERE) | Full (custom Select) |[0m[0m
| Multiple relations | One call per relation, each re-joins parent | Load many relations from same parent vec |[0m[0m
| Return type | [38;5;10mVec<(Parent, Vec<Child>)>[0m | [38;5;10mVec<Vec<Child>>[0m (aligned to input) |[0m[0m
[0m[0m
For your project's pattern (e.g., loading contratos for a list of propiedades), [38;5;10mload_many[0m is typically better since you already have the parent models and may need to load multiple relations (contratos, gastos, mantenimiento) without redundant JOINs."}
